title: Education
description: Vocabulary for academic settings, studying, and education systems

vocabulary.meaning: The subjects and content taught in an educational programme
vocabulary.example: The school updated its science curriculum last year.

vocabulary.meaning: A long essay required for a university degree
vocabulary.example: She spent a year writing her doctoral dissertation.

vocabulary.meaning: Financial aid awarded to a student based on merit or need
vocabulary.example: He received a full scholarship to study abroad.

vocabulary.meaning: The fee paid for instruction at a school or university
vocabulary.example: University tuition has risen significantly.

vocabulary.meaning: A half-year term in an academic year
vocabulary.example: The spring semester starts in February.

vocabulary.meaning: An official record of a student's grades
vocabulary.example: Please provide an official transcript with your application.

vocabulary.meaning: A course required before another can be taken
vocabulary.example: Calculus is a prerequisite for this physics course.

vocabulary.meaning: An outline of the topics covered in a course
vocabulary.example: The professor distributed the syllabus on the first day.

vocabulary.meaning: A research paper presenting an original argument
vocabulary.example: His thesis explored AI in healthcare.

vocabulary.meaning: Copying someone else's work and claiming it as your own
vocabulary.example: Plagiarism can result in expulsion from university.

vocabulary.meaning: Grade Point Average ‚ÄĒ a measure of academic performance
vocabulary.example: She maintained a 3.9 GPA throughout university.

vocabulary.meaning: Activities done outside the regular school programme
vocabulary.example: Extracurricular activities develop teamwork and leadership.

vocabulary.meaning: An optional course chosen by the student
vocabulary.example: She chose photography as her elective this term.

vocabulary.meaning: A formal talk given to students by a teacher
vocabulary.example: The professor's lecture on climate change was fascinating.

vocabulary.meaning: A small group class for discussion and learning
vocabulary.example: The weekly seminar focuses on case studies.

vocabulary.meaning: The head of a faculty or college within a university
vocabulary.example: She was elected Dean of the School of Business.

vocabulary.meaning: Official recognition that a school meets quality standards
vocabulary.translation: c√īng nhŠļ≠n kiŠĽÉm ńĎŠĽčnh chŠļ•t l∆įŠĽ£ng
vocabulary.example: The university received international accreditation.

vocabulary.meaning: A grant or position awarded to a researcher or student
vocabulary.example: He was awarded a Fulbright Fellowship.

vocabulary.meaning: A student studying for their first university degree
vocabulary.example: Undergraduate students study for three to four years.

vocabulary.meaning: A student pursuing a degree beyond a bachelor's
vocabulary.example: She's doing postgraduate research in neuroscience.

vocabulary.meaning: To officially register for a course or programme
vocabulary.example: You must enroll before the deadline.

vocabulary.meaning: The ceremony marking the completion of a degree
vocabulary.example: His graduation ceremony is next month.

vocabulary.meaning: The act of being present at class or school
vocabulary.example: Attendance is mandatory for all lab sessions.

vocabulary.meaning: A task given to students to complete outside class
vocabulary.example: The assignment is due next Monday.

vocabulary.meaning: Commitment to honesty in all academic work
vocabulary.example: Academic integrity is fundamental to our institution.

vocabulary.meaning: The grounds and buildings of a university or college
vocabulary.example: The campus has state-of-the-art research facilities.

vocabulary.meaning: A period of practical work experience in a company
vocabulary.example: She completed an internship at Google last summer.

vocabulary.meaning: Evaluation of work by others with similar expertise
vocabulary.example: The research paper went through peer review before publication.

vocabulary.meaning: Scheduled times when a professor is available to students
vocabulary.example: Come during office hours to discuss your essay.

vocabulary.meaning: Relating to mental processes of thinking and learning
vocabulary.example: Early education supports cognitive development.

vocabulary.meaning: The ability to read and write
vocabulary.example: Digital literacy is now a core educational goal.

vocabulary.meaning: A training programme combining work and education
vocabulary.example: He completed a plumbing apprenticeship.

vocabulary.meaning: The method and practice of teaching
vocabulary.example: Modern pedagogy emphasizes student-centered learning.

vocabulary.meaning: Designed to help students who are struggling
vocabulary.example: He joined a remedial English class to improve.

vocabulary.meaning: The schedule of terms, holidays and exams for a school year
vocabulary.example: Check the academic calendar for exam dates.

vocabulary.meaning: Funds provided for academic research projects
vocabulary.example: She won a $50,000 research grant.

vocabulary.meaning: Analyzing information objectively to form judgments
vocabulary.example: Critical thinking is a key skill taught at university.

vocabulary.meaning: A group of students who meet to study together
vocabulary.example: Our study group meets every Thursday evening.

vocabulary.meaning: The final date for submitting an assignment
vocabulary.example: The essay deadline is Friday at midnight.

vocabulary.meaning: A person who gives private or small group teaching
vocabulary.translation: gia s∆į, giŠļ£ng vi√™n h∆įŠĽõng dŠļęn
vocabulary.example: She hired a tutor to help with mathematics.

vocabulary.meaning: Stress experienced by students related to academic performance
vocabulary.example: Academic pressure can affect student mental health.

vocabulary.meaning: Education delivered through electronic means
vocabulary.example: E-learning became essential during the pandemic.

vocabulary.meaning: A place where books and resources are kept for study
vocabulary.example: The university library is open 24 hours during exams.

vocabulary.meaning: An exam or assessment at the middle of a term
vocabulary.example: The midterm exam covers chapters one through five.

vocabulary.meaning: A comprehensive exam at the end of a course
vocabulary.example: The final exam counts for 40% of the grade.

vocabulary.meaning: A mark given to assess a student's performance
vocabulary.example: She received an A grade on her history paper.

vocabulary.meaning: A person who leaves school or university before finishing
vocabulary.example: The school implemented programs to reduce dropout rates.

vocabulary.meaning: The process of being accepted into a school or university
vocabulary.example: University admission requires strong academic results.

vocabulary.meaning: Education focused on skills for a specific trade or career
vocabulary.example: Vocational training prepares students for practical work.

vocabulary.meaning: The student with the highest academic rank in a graduating class
vocabulary.example: She was named valedictorian of her graduating class.

vocabulary.meaning: A former student of a school or university
vocabulary.example: He is a proud alumnus of Harvard University.

vocabulary.meaning: A permanent position granted to a professor after review
vocabulary.example: She received tenure after six years of teaching and research.

vocabulary.meaning: A senior academic officer in a university
vocabulary.translation: ph√≥ hiŠĽáu tr∆įŠĽüng phŠĽ• tr√°ch hŠĽćc vŠĽ•
vocabulary.example: The provost oversees all academic programs at the university.

vocabulary.meaning: A Latin honor meaning 'with great praise' for high academic achievement
vocabulary.example: She graduated magna cum laude from the law school.

vocabulary.meaning: The formal process of entering a university as a student
vocabulary.example: Matriculation day marks the official start of university life.

vocabulary.meaning: A period of paid leave for a professor to pursue research
vocabulary.example: The professor took a sabbatical to write his book.

vocabulary.meaning: A graduation ceremony at a university
vocabulary.example: The commencement speaker inspired the graduating class.

vocabulary.meaning: A list of students with outstanding academic performance
vocabulary.translation: danh s√°ch sinh vi√™n xuŠļ•t sŠļĮc
vocabulary.example: She made the dean's list every semester.

vocabulary.meaning: A staff member who guides students on course selection
vocabulary.example: Meet with your academic advisor to plan your courses.

vocabulary.meaning: The assignments and tasks completed during a course
vocabulary.example: Coursework accounts for 60% of the final grade.

vocabulary.meaning: A formal presentation where a student defends their research
vocabulary.example: Her dissertation defense lasted two hours.

vocabulary.meaning: A reference to a source used in academic writing
vocabulary.example: Every claim must be supported by a proper citation.

vocabulary.meaning: A brief summary of a research paper or article
vocabulary.example: Read the abstract to understand the paper's main findings.

vocabulary.meaning: A conference where experts present and discuss research
vocabulary.example: She presented her findings at an international symposium.

vocabulary.meaning: A proposed explanation to be tested through research
vocabulary.example: The experiment was designed to test the hypothesis.

vocabulary.meaning: The system of methods used in a particular area of study
vocabulary.example: The methodology section explains how the study was conducted.

vocabulary.meaning: Based on observation and experiment rather than theory
vocabulary.example: The research provides empirical evidence for the theory.

vocabulary.meaning: Research based on descriptions and interpretations rather than numbers
vocabulary.example: Qualitative research often uses interviews and case studies.

vocabulary.meaning: Research based on numerical data and statistical analysis
vocabulary.example: Quantitative methods were used to analyze the survey results.

vocabulary.meaning: A detailed document listing academic and professional history
vocabulary.example: Submit your curriculum vitae when applying for the position.

vocabulary.meaning: An official document certifying completion of a course of study
vocabulary.example: She proudly displayed her diploma on the wall.

vocabulary.meaning: An introductory programme for new students
vocabulary.example: Freshman orientation helps students adjust to university life.

vocabulary.meaning: A publication featuring peer-reviewed research articles
vocabulary.example: Her article was published in a top academic journal.

vocabulary.meaning: Software that checks work for copied content
vocabulary.translation: phŠļßn mŠĽĀm ph√°t hiŠĽán ńĎŠļ°o vńÉn
vocabulary.example: The university uses a plagiarism detector for all submissions.

vocabulary.meaning: Money provided to students to help pay for education
vocabulary.example: She applied for financial aid to cover tuition costs.

vocabulary.meaning: A programme allowing students to study in a foreign country
vocabulary.example: He spent a semester on a study abroad program in Japan.

vocabulary.meaning: A student attending a partner institution in another country
vocabulary.example: The exchange student from Germany joined our class.

vocabulary.meaning: A residential building for students at a university
vocabulary.example: Most freshmen live in the dormitory on campus.

vocabulary.meaning: A retired professor who retains an honorary title
vocabulary.example: The professor emeritus still gives occasional guest lectures.

vocabulary.meaning: A condition that affects a person's ability to learn
vocabulary.example: The school provides support for students with learning disabilities.

vocabulary.meaning: A set of criteria for grading an assignment
vocabulary.example: The teacher shared the rubric so students knew how they would be graded.

vocabulary.meaning: An exam administered and scored in a consistent manner
vocabulary.example: The SAT is a standardized test used for college admissions.

vocabulary.meaning: A list recognizing students with high grades
vocabulary.example: She was on the honor roll for three consecutive years.

vocabulary.meaning: A large room designed for lectures with many students
vocabulary.example: The lecture hall seats over 300 students.

vocabulary.meaning: The primary subject a student specializes in at university
vocabulary.example: She chose computer science as her major.

vocabulary.meaning: A secondary subject studied alongside the main degree
vocabulary.example: He has a major in economics and a minor in statistics.

vocabulary.meaning: A unit measuring the amount of instruction in a course
vocabulary.example: This course is worth three credit hours.

vocabulary.meaning: A warning period for students with poor academic performance
vocabulary.translation: thŠĽĚi gian cŠļ£nh c√°o hŠĽćc vŠĽ•
vocabulary.example: Students on academic probation must improve their grades.

vocabulary.meaning: Learning activities pursued after formal education
vocabulary.example: Many professionals take continuing education courses.

vocabulary.meaning: A school where students live during the academic term
vocabulary.example: He attended a prestigious boarding school in England.

vocabulary.meaning: Education where students study remotely from the institution
vocabulary.example: Distance learning allows students to study from home.

vocabulary.meaning: The permanent removal of a student from a school
vocabulary.example: Cheating on exams can lead to expulsion.

vocabulary.meaning: A temporary removal of a student from school as punishment
vocabulary.example: He received a three-day suspension for fighting.

vocabulary.meaning: A break period during the school day for rest or play
vocabulary.example: Children play outside during recess.

vocabulary.meaning: A community of former students connected to their school
vocabulary.example: The alumni network provides valuable career connections.

vocabulary.meaning: A fund of money donated to a university for long-term support
vocabulary.example: Harvard's endowment exceeds $50 billion.

vocabulary.meaning: The schedule of an educational institution's terms and holidays
vocabulary.example: The academic calendar runs from September to June.

vocabulary.meaning: An optional subject a student can choose to study
vocabulary.example: I chose photography as an elective course.

vocabulary.meaning: A student elected to represent a class in discussions
vocabulary.example: She was elected class representative for the second year.

vocabulary.meaning: A policy allowing students to enrol without selective admission
vocabulary.translation: tuyŠĽÉn sinh mŠĽü kh√īng hŠļ°n chŠļŅ
vocabulary.example: The community college operates on open enrolment.

quiz.question: What is a 'curriculum'?
options: The subjects and content taught in a programme

quiz.question: What is 'plagiarism'?
options: Copying someone else's work without credit

quiz.question: What is a 'prerequisite'?
options: A course required before another can be taken

quiz.question: What is 'accreditation'?
options: Official recognition that a school meets quality standards

quiz.question: What is an 'elective'?
options: An optional course chosen by the student

quiz.question: What does 'enroll' mean?
options: To officially register for a course

quiz.question: What is a 'dissertation'?
options: A long essay required for a university degree

quiz.question: What is 'peer review'?
options: Evaluation of work by people with similar expertise

quiz.question: What is a 'seminar'?
options: A large lecture with hundreds of students
A small group class for discussion

quiz.question: What is a 'valedictorian'?
options: The student with the highest academic rank in a graduating class

quiz.question: What is a 'rubric'?
options: A set of criteria for grading an assignment

quiz.question: What does 'tenure' mean for a professor?
options: A permanent position granted after review

quiz.question: What is a 'hypothesis' in research?
options: The final conclusion of a study
A proposed explanation to be tested through research

quiz.question: What is an 'alumni network'?
options: A community of former students connected to their school
